Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.

For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
From the last action:
- Orange square moved from [12,24] to [14,27] - moved DOWN and RIGHT
- The path has white diamonds going toward the orange ring at bottom
- Purple square still at [41,49]
So clicking magenta on orange square moves it DOWN-RIGHT toward the orange ring. This matches the Level 1 pattern where clicking magenta moves the square toward the ring of the same color.
Now I need to figure out:
1. How many magenta clicks to move orange square to orange ring
2. How many magenta clicks to move purple square to purple ring
3. Whether both need to reach their rings for level completion
Looking at the path:
- Orange square at [14,27], white diamonds at [15,24], [16,22], [17,19], [18,16], [19,14], [20,11]... wait those go UP-LEFT
- But also white diamonds at [12,36], [13,32], [11,40]... those go RIGHT
- And [12,28]... 
Hmm, the path seems more complex. Let me look at the white diamonds more carefully:
- [11,40] w pixels=5
- [12,36] w pixels=4
- [13,32] w pixels=4
- [12,28] w pixels=2
- [15,24] w pixels=2
- [16,22] w pixels=2
- [17,19] w pixels=3
- [18,16] w pixels=3
- [19,14] w pixels=2
- [20,11] w pixels=3
The orange square moved from [12,24] to [14,27]. The white diamonds near it are at [12,28], [13,32], [12,36], [11,40]. These form a path going RIGHT from the square's original position.
But there are also white diamonds going LEFT from the original position: [15,24], [16,22], [17,19], etc. These seem to be part of a different path.
Wait, I think there might be TWO paths for the orange square:
1. Path going RIGHT toward the orange ring
2. Path going LEFT toward... somewhere else
And the purple square has:
- White diamonds at [37,46], [38,47] (above it) going toward endpoint [35,45]
- White diamonds at [44,51], [45,52], [46,53] (below it) going toward endpoint [48,54]
Hmm, but in Level 1, the goal was to move the square toward the ring. So:
- Orange square → orange ring (bottom area)
- Purple square → purple ring (right side)
Let me just keep clicking magenta on both squares and see what happens. The orange square moved DOWN-RIGHT, which is toward the orange ring. Let me continue clicking.
Actually, let me think about the path structure more carefully. The orange square is at [14,27] now. The white diamonds going right are at [12,28], [13,32], [12,36], [11,40]. These don't seem to form a straight path to the orange ring at [48-54, 37-43].
Wait, maybe the path isn't straight. Let me just keep clicking magenta and see where the orange square goes.
For the purple square, it's at [41,49]. The purple ring is at [15-21, 54-59]. The white diamonds near it are at [37,46], [38,47] (above) and [44,51], [45,52], [46,53] (below). To reach the purple ring, it would need to go UP-RIGHT.
Let me try clicking magenta on the purple square to see which direction it moves.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: Orange square moved from [12,24] to [14,27] (DOWN-RIGHT toward orange ring). Let me test the purple square's direction too.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Test purple square magenta click
# Purple square at [41,49], purple ring at [15-21, 54-59]
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 41, 'col': 49}])
print(f"Purple magenta click: done={result.get('done')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['color'] in ['p', 'M', 'O']:
        if node['pixels'] >= 20 or node['color'] == 'M':
            cr = sum(p[0] for p in node['boundary']) / len(node['boundary'])
            cc = sum(p[1] for p in node['boundary']) / len(node['boundary'])
            print(f"  {node['color']} pixels={node['pixels']} center≈({cr:.0f},{cc:.0f})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
Purple magenta click: done=False
  O pixels=20 center≈(14,27)
  M pixels=1 center≈(14,27)
  p pixels=20 center≈(41,49)
  M pixels=1 center≈(41,49)
